fundamentals.name: Capital One Financial Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Capital One Financial Corporation operates as the financial services holding company for the Capital One, National Association, which engages in the provision of various financial products and services in the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. It operates through three segments: Credit Card, Consumer Banking, and Commercial Banking. The company accepts checking accounts, money market deposits, negotiable order of withdrawals, savings deposits, time deposits, and sweep accounts. Its loan products include credit card and personal loans; auto and retail banking loans; and commercial and multifamily real estate, and commercial and industrial loans. The company offers credit and debit card products; bank lending; and provides advisory, capital markets, net interchange, treasury management, and depository services. It serves consumers, small businesses, and commercial clients through digital channels, branches, cafés, and other distribution channels located in New York, Louisiana, Texas, Maryland, Virginia, New Jersey, and the District of Columbia. Capital One Financial Corporation was founded in 1988 and is headquartered in McLean, Virginia.

judgment.summary: A large financial institution with strong revenue growth and a low forward P/E, but facing headwinds in earnings growth and returns.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed consensus by 3.3%; next earnings date is 2026-07-21.
positives: Forward P/E of 7.79x is very attractive relative to trailing P/E of 57.86x and PEG of 0.20.
Revenue growth of 46.3% YoY is exceptionally strong.
Quarterly earnings growth of 54.8% indicates recent positive momentum.
Total cash of $79.39B provides significant liquidity.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target of $255.19.
negatives: Earnings growth YoY is negative at -3.2%.
Profit margin of 8.9% and Return on Equity of 3.3% are modest.
Dividend payout ratio of 86.1% is high, potentially limiting future increases.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 3.3%.

news.paragraph: Capital One Financial Corporation (COF) saw its investors reject a golden parachute proposal, with risks remaining a focus. Separately, the company's dividend and Discover integration are being viewed as a potential new capital allocation playbook. The stock is also mentioned in relation to analyst sentiment, with one report from TD Cowen and another from Goldman Sachs focusing on AI infrastructure bets, though COF itself is not the primary subject of the latter. The upcoming earnings date on July 21st will be key for further clarity.
